default.hero: A year-by-year guide connecting courses, internships, and technical skill-building for your path in Computer Science.
default.highlight: This roadmap is a starting point — a guide to the classes, technical skills, and recruiting timelines worth thinking about as you navigate a career in software engineering, AI, systems, or data.

classes.popupText: Introductory computer science course covering programming fundamentals.
Course on how computers work at the hardware and systems level.

freshman.hero: Freshman year is about starting the CS sequence and learning the foundations of programming and computational thinking.
freshman.highlight: Best use of freshman year: complete CS15X early, build strong programming fundamentals, and start exploring different areas of computer science such as software engineering, AI, systems, and data.

classes.popupText: Introductory programming and computer science fundamentals course.

classes.text: MA 125 / MA 130 / MA 160 (math foundation depending on placement)

Dartmouth’s Tuck Business Bridge Program, it is a short, intensive pre-MBA business program for undergraduates and recent graduates that teaches core business skills, includes a capstone project and career guidance, and is designed to help launch internships and jobs.

internships.popupText: Google's freshman and sophomore internship focused on early software engineering development.
Microsoft program designed for early college students exploring software engineering.
Capital One offers paid data analyst internships for undergraduates — work on real customer data, learn SQL/Python, and explore analytics career paths.

projects.popupText: Guided series: color flipper, counter, navbar, modal, tabs, slider, and more—good for DOM practice and portfolio pieces.
roadmap.sh spec: one-page résumé in semantic HTML—great first public GitHub Pages deploy.
Extend a simple HTML site into a styled portfolio (responsive layout, typography, hosting options).
Small game loop project: random number, user input, loops—fits any intro language (Python/Java/C).
Track income/expenses in the terminal—practice file I/O or simple persistence.
To-do list in the terminal—foundation for later REST APIs and databases.
Simple UI + conversion logic—good bridge between HTML/CSS and light JavaScript.
Fetch and display a user’s public events—first taste of JSON, HTTP, and an API key not required.
Full free curriculum with hands-on projects leading into HTML/CSS/JS and Git.
Free 6-month Coursera certificate covering spreadsheets, SQL, Python, Tableau, and data storytelling — strong foundational credential for data-curious freshmen.

projects.text: Kaggle Learn: Intro to Python + Pandas
projects.popupText: Free short courses on Python fundamentals and Pandas data manipulation — the building blocks for any data science work.
Free bite-sized certifications in product prioritization, experimentation, and AI — a low-commitment way to explore product management early.

projects.popupText: Multi-chapter build: Flask, SQLAlchemy, auth, email, migrations—classic full-stack progression.

roadmap.sh spec for CRUD over HTTP—practice models, validation, and API design.
Proxy/aggregate weather data behind your own API—practice env vars, HTTP client, JSON.
Structured project: posts, routing, styling—strong portfolio centerpiece for sophomores.
Huge curated list of free APIs—pick one domain and build a small client or dashboard.
Deploy a static site with GitHub Actions—professional practice for shipping frontend work.
Not a single app, but the shell, Git, debugging, and metadata skills that make every project faster.

projects.text: Kaggle: Titanic — Machine Learning from Disaster
projects.popupText: Classic beginner ML competition — predict survival using logistic regression and random forests. Great for learning data cleaning, EDA, and feature engineering.
Beginner regression competition practicing feature engineering, random forests, and gradient boosting on real housing data.
Free 4-hour course covering Seaborn — line charts, bar charts, heatmaps, scatter plots, and distributions. Earn a certificate.
Turn a Python analysis into an interactive web app in under an hour — impressive portfolio piece and no frontend skills required.

projects.popupText: Pick a product you use and write a PRD for a new feature — covers user research, problem framing, metrics, and wireframes. Core PM skill-builder.

Extremely selective 12-week PM internship for rising seniors — own a feature end-to-end through ideation, implementation, and metrics analysis. Pipeline to Google's full-time APM program.

12-week paid internship ($54-$60/hr) turning business data into insights for product design and customer acquisition using SQL, Python, and ML.
10-week internship working with large music and user behavior datasets — build dashboards, run A/B tests, and communicate data-driven insights.
12-week immersive internship building statistical models and analytics solutions for financial institutions, with mentorship and leadership presentations.
PM internship at Amazon — work with engineering, design, and data science teams on customer-facing product challenges at massive scale.
10-week paid internship ($47-$52/hr) working on customer behavior analytics, ML models, and business decision support. Strong pipeline to full-time roles.

projects.popupText: Design URLs, persistence, and redirects—common interview-style system with clear extensions.
Cart, products, payments—forces relational modeling, transactions, and error handling.
HTTP cache in front of another origin—touches sockets, caching policy, and concurrency.
Editor + storage + preview—shows full-stack polish and file or DB sync.
API integration, async loading, and UI state—good React/Vue practice.
Users, sessions, progress—realistic domain modeling for health/fitness apps.
Short course leading to a simple model—pair with a Kaggle competition or notebook portfolio.
Data cleaning and analysis—foundation for DS/ML projects and data-heavy SWE roles.
Call models from code—build a small CLI or web tool; document costs and safety.
Curated OSS projects tagged for newcomers—pick one issue and ship a real PR.
Free 10-hour course covering missing values, categorical variables, pipelines, cross-validation, XGBoost, and data leakage.
Train a model, build a Streamlit or Flask front-end, and deploy to the cloud — shows full-stack data science capability to recruiters.
Analyze a product you use — identify user pain points, propose improvements, define success metrics, and create wireframes. Core PM portfolio piece.
Design and analyze a mock A/B test — define hypothesis, sample size, statistical significance, and business recommendation. Valued by both DS and PM roles.
Curated project list from beginner to advanced — job scraper, sentiment analysis, recommendation engine, and more.

Job board for roles at Y Combinator-backed startups.
Two-year rotational PM program at Google (1-2% acceptance rate) — 2-3 rotations across products, $140K-$180K total comp.
18-month program with 3 rotations across Meta products, includes bootcamp and global research trip. $160K-$200K total comp. No CS degree required.
Three rotations across Uber product verticals — includes 2-week boot camp, global research trip, and mentorship from senior PMs.

full_time_roles.text: Stripe New Grad Product Manager Accelerator
full_time_roles.popupText: 12-month intensive PM training at Stripe — requires CS or engineering degree, $120K-$180K base salary. Full-time PM role after completion.
Two-year rotational APM program at LinkedIn with mentorship and cross-functional product ownership experience.

full_time_roles.text: JPMorgan Chase Data Science Analyst Program
full_time_roles.popupText: Full-time analyst program focused on machine learning, business intelligence, data engineering, and visualization. Requires Python proficiency.
New grad data scientist role at a leading data/AI company — $140K-$150K. Requires strong SQL, Python, and ML fundamentals.
Full-time rotational program covering analytics, ML, and business strategy — values quantitative backgrounds and Python/SQL skills.

projects.popupText: roadmap.sh advanced track: services, boundaries, and scaling concerns—capstone-level.
Ranking, concurrency, and live updates—good WebSocket/event practice.
Seats, inventory, and contention—classic domain for transactions and race conditions.
Backup/restore workflows—ops credibility and scripting depth.
Metrics, dashboards, alerts—must-have for SRE/platform-minded portfolios.
Zero-downtime releases—pairs well with any web app you already built.
Step-by-step guides for shells, Git, databases, compilers—pick one deep dive for a standout story.
Incremental TCP server, protocol parsing—strong systems interview prep (check current pricing/terms).
Implement Git objects and plumbing—deepens understanding of version control internals.
Train a production model, build an interactive Streamlit front-end, and deploy — capstone-level demonstration of full-stack data science capability.

default.hero: A year-by-year guide connecting courses, opportunities, and practical next steps for your path in Economics.
default.highlight: This roadmap is a starting point — a guide to the classes, programs, and resources worth thinking about as you navigate a career in Economics.

classes.popupText: Introduces the fundamental problem in economics: limited resources to satisfy unlimited needs and wants. Concentration on how markets allocate these scarce resources and when they fail to operate efficiently. After analyzing costs, students look at how firms in market structures ranging from perfectly competitive to monopolistic make decisions. Key principles are illustrated by applying them to current economic issues. Students will use standard economic models to describe market structures and the effects of policy interventions, solve problems using graphical or algebraic models of these markets, and choose an appropriate model to analyze economic events described in news articles.

Introduces the measurement of macroeconomic variables and basic theoretical models of aggregate economic behavior. Focuses on the study of fluctuations in economic activity, long-run economic growth, and the role of monetary and fiscal policy in achieving macroeconomic goals. Students will develop their analytical problem-solving skills, hone their ability to think critically, gain experience in building and understanding theoretical models, and sharpen their capacity to understand and critique macroeconomic policy.
The theory of the pricing, distribution, and allocation of resources in a market economy. Emphasis placed on the various meanings of economic efficiency.

Devoted to the development and examination of various theoretical frameworks to explain fluctuations in output, interest rates, exchange rates, unemployment, inflation, and economic growth in a globally interdependent economy. Continued study of the theoretical development of macroeconomic models and further refinement of understanding the effectiveness and optimality of macroeconomic policy. Students gain an understanding of the importance of expectations, the determination of asset prices (e.g., bond and stock prices), the relationship between financial markets and the macroeconomy, and the implications and limitations of models and policies.

Provides students with the fundamental ability to understand and carry out research in economics. Covers the use of basic statistical methods, probability, and regression analysis in the description and interpretation of economic data. Students practice the application of these techniques working with powerful statistical software.
Advanced economics topics and capstone seminar work.

programs.popupText: Programs introducing early college students to finance, consulting, or economics careers.
Industry internships that often lead to full-time job offers.

freshman.hero: Freshman year is mainly about getting the intro sequence started and beginning to understand what the major looks like.
freshman.highlight: Best use of freshman year: complete EC133 and EC134, start building your foundation early, and get familiar with finance, policy, consulting, and research paths before recruiting ramps up later.

classes.popupText: Introduces the fundamental problem in economics: limited resources to satisfy unlimited needs and wants. Concentration on how markets allocate these scarce resources and when they fail to operate efficiently. After analyzing costs, students look at how firms in market structures ranging from perfectly competitive to monopolistic make decisions. Key principles are illustrated by applying them to current economic issues. Students will use standard economic models to describe market structures and the effects of policy interventions, solve problems using graphical or algebraic models of these markets, and choose an appropriate model to analyze economic events described in news articles.

Introduces the measurement of macroeconomic variables and basic theoretical models of aggregate economic behavior. Focuses on the study of fluctuations in economic activity, long-run economic growth, and the role of monetary and fiscal policy in achieving macroeconomic goals. Students will develop their analytical problem-solving skills, hone their ability to think critically, gain experience in building and understanding theoretical models, and sharpen their capacity to understand and critique macroeconomic policy.

sophomore.hero: Sophomore year is where the major becomes more structured — theory, methods, and math become much more important.
sophomore.highlight: Best use of sophomore year: finish the core theory and methods work so junior year can focus on stronger electives, technical development, and internship preparation.

classes.popupText: Devoted to the development and examination of various theoretical frameworks to explain fluctuations in output, interest rates, exchange rates, unemployment, inflation, and economic growth in a globally interdependent economy. Continued study of the theoretical development of macroeconomic models and further refinement of understanding the effectiveness and optimality of macroeconomic policy. Students gain an understanding of the importance of expectations, the determination of asset prices (e.g., bond and stock prices), the relationship between financial markets and the macroeconomy, and the implications and limitations of models and policies.
The theory of the pricing, distribution, and allocation of resources in a market economy. Emphasis placed on the various meanings of economic efficiency.

junior.hero: Junior year is usually the most important year for connecting coursework to internships, recruiting, and upper-level economics work.
junior.highlight: Best use of junior year: pair upper-level econ classes with serious internship prep, stronger technical skill building, and a more focused recruiting strategy.

classes.popupText: Upper-level economics course in a specialization of interest.

An introduction to quantitative methods used for the analysis of economic phenomenon, covering the theoretical development of the ordinary least squares regression framework, tools for model specification and estimation, hypothesis testing, methods for correcting errors in parameter estimation, and the analysis of econometric results in the context of a wide range of empirical applications. Through lab exercises and a final empirical project, develops model-building skills, builds confidence in applying econometric methods to real-world data, deepens the understanding of statistical inference, and improves the capacity for communicating econometric results.
